title: Jehovah-Rophi. I Am The Lord That Healeth Thee. - Exodus xv.26.
content:
Heal us, Emmanuel, here we are,
    Waiting to feel thy touch:
    Deep-wounded souls to thee repair,
    And, Saviour, we are such.


    Our faith is feeble, we confess,
    We faintly trust thy word;
    But wilt thou pity us the less?
    Be that far from thee, Lord!


    Remember him who once applied,
    With trembling, for relief;
    �Lord, I believe,� with tears he cried,[1]
    �Oh, help my unbelief!�


    She too, who touch�d thee in the press,
    And healing virtue stole,
    Was answer�d, �Daughter, go in peace,[2]
    Thy faith hath made thee whole.�


    Conceal�d amid the gathering throng,
    She would have shunn�d thy view;
    And if her faith was firm and strong,
    Had strong misgivings too.


    Like her, with hopes and fears we come,
    To touch thee, if we may;
    Oh! send us not despairing home,
    Send none unheal�d away.
meaning:
William Cowper's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Jehovah-Rophi. I Am The Lord That Healeth Thee. - Exodus xv.26."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
